title: I lost money in a UPI fraud — what should I do right now?
content: If money has been debited from your account due to a UPI fraud or scam, act immediately because the first few hours are critical for recovering funds. First, call the National Cyber Crime Helpline 1930 right away and report the fraudulent transaction; this is a toll-free Government of India helpline operated under the Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C). Second, call Pratham Bank's 24x7 customer care at 1800-123-4567 and ask the agent to immediately block your UPI ID and flag the disputed transaction so the beneficiary account can be frozen before the money is withdrawn. Third, register a complaint online at the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, cybercrime.gov.in, and note down the acknowledgement number. Keep your transaction reference number (UTR), the amount, the date and time, and the UPI ID or phone number of the fraudster ready, as these details speed up the investigation. The sooner you report, the higher the chance the bank can put a hold on the money in the fraudster's account.

title: What is the 1930 cyber crime helpline and how does it help?
content: 1930 is the National Cyber Crime Helpline number set up by the Government of India through the Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C). When you call 1930 to report financial fraud such as a UPI scam, debit card fraud, or fake payment link, your complaint is routed through the Citizen Financial Cyber Fraud Reporting and Management System. This system can alert the banks and payment platforms involved and attempt to put a hold on the defrauded amount before it is withdrawn by the criminal. Calling 1930 quickly, ideally within the 'golden hour' of realising the fraud, gives the best chance of freezing and recovering your money. After calling 1930 you should also file a detailed complaint at cybercrime.gov.in and inform your bank.

title: How do I block my UPI ID after a fraud?
content: To block or disable your UPI ID after a suspected fraud, you have several options. In the Pratham Bank mobile app, go to the UPI section, tap on Manage UPI, select your UPI ID, and choose Deactivate or Block UPI. You can also call Pratham Bank customer care at 1800-123-4567, choose the fraud and security option in the menu, and ask the agent to block all your UPI IDs linked to your account. If you use a third-party app such as BHIM, Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm, you should also remove or deactivate your bank account from within that app. Blocking the UPI ID stops any further debit through UPI while you investigate. Once the issue is resolved and your account is secure, you can re-register a fresh UPI ID and set a new UPI PIN.

title: How do I raise a dispute or chargeback for a fraudulent UPI transaction?
content: To formally dispute a fraudulent or unauthorised UPI transaction, raise a complaint with Pratham Bank within three days for the fastest resolution. You can raise the dispute through the mobile app under Help, then Report a Transaction, by calling customer care at 1800-123-4567, or by visiting your branch. The bank will register the dispute on the NPCI dispute redressal system (UDIR), investigate with the beneficiary bank, and respond. As per RBI's limited liability rules, if you report an unauthorised electronic transaction within three working days of receiving the communication, your liability is generally zero, provided the fraud was not due to your own negligence such as sharing your UPI PIN or OTP. Delay in reporting can increase your liability, so always report immediately. Keep your complaint reference number safe for follow-up.

title: Will I get my money back? RBI rules on unauthorised transactions
content: The Reserve Bank of India has a customer protection framework on limiting liability in unauthorised electronic banking transactions. If the unauthorised transaction happened due to a fault or negligence of the bank, your liability is zero regardless of whether you reported it. If it happened due to a third-party breach where neither the bank nor you are at fault, and you notify the bank within three working days of receiving the transaction communication, your liability is zero. If you notify within four to seven working days, your liability is limited to a capped amount depending on the account type. If you delay beyond seven working days, the liability is decided as per the bank's board-approved policy. Importantly, if the loss is due to your own negligence, such as sharing your UPI PIN, OTP, CVV, or password, you may bear the full loss until you report it. This is why you should never share these credentials and should report fraud immediately.

title: What are common UPI scams I should watch out for?
content: Common UPI scams in India include: the 'request money' trap, where a fraudster sends you a collect request and convinces you to approve it by entering your UPI PIN, even though you never need to enter your PIN to receive money; fake customer care numbers found on search engines that lead you to scammers pretending to be bank staff; QR code scams where you are asked to scan a code to receive money, but scanning and entering your PIN actually sends money out; screen-sharing app scams where a fraudster asks you to install AnyDesk, TeamViewer, or QuickSupport and then watches your screen and credentials; fake KYC update messages with malicious links; fake job, lottery, electricity bill, or loan offers; and SIM swap or OTP theft. Remember the golden rule: you never need to enter your UPI PIN to receive money. Only enter your PIN when you are sending money.

title: What should I never share to stay safe from UPI fraud?
content: To stay safe from UPI and banking fraud, never share your UPI PIN, MPIN, OTP, CVV, debit or credit card number, expiry date, internet banking password, or any one-time codes with anyone, including people claiming to be from the bank, RBI, or government. Pratham Bank and the Reserve Bank of India will never call, message, or email you asking for your PIN, OTP, password, or full card number. Never install screen-sharing or remote-access apps such as AnyDesk, TeamViewer, or QuickSupport on the instructions of an unknown caller. Do not click on suspicious links promising KYC updates, refunds, rewards, or prizes. Always verify the official customer care number from the bank's official website or the back of your debit card rather than from a search engine. Enable transaction alerts and review your statements regularly.

title: Can the bank freeze my account if there is suspicious activity?
content: Yes. If you suspect your account has been compromised in a fraud, you can ask Pratham Bank to temporarily freeze or put a hold on your account to prevent further unauthorised transactions. Call customer care at 1800-123-4567 and request a temporary debit freeze, or visit your branch. The bank may also proactively freeze an account or block transactions if its fraud monitoring systems detect suspicious activity, to protect your funds. A freeze stops outgoing transactions while you and the bank investigate. Once the issue is resolved and your credentials are reset, the bank can lift the freeze. During this period, incoming credits usually continue to work. Always keep your registered mobile number active so you receive alerts and verification calls.

title: How do I set or reset my UPI PIN?
content: To set or reset your UPI PIN, open your UPI app such as the Pratham Bank app, BHIM, Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm, go to the UPI settings or the bank account linked for UPI, and select Set UPI PIN or Reset UPI PIN. You will be asked to enter the last six digits and the expiry date of your debit card linked to that bank account. An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number. After entering the OTP and card details, you can set a new four or six digit UPI PIN. If you have forgotten your UPI PIN, use the Forgot UPI PIN or Reset option and follow the same debit card and OTP verification steps. You need an active debit card and your registered mobile number to set a UPI PIN. Never share your UPI PIN with anyone.

title: What is the daily UPI transaction limit?
content: The standard UPI transaction limit set by NPCI is up to one lakh rupees per transaction for most person-to-person and merchant payments, with a maximum of twenty transactions per day per bank account, though individual banks may set their own limits within this. For certain categories such as payments to verified merchants, capital markets, insurance, and hospitals or educational institutions, NPCI has allowed higher per-transaction limits of up to five lakh rupees. For new UPI users, there is often a lower limit of five thousand rupees in the first twenty-four hours after registration as a safety measure. Pratham Bank's default UPI limit is one lakh rupees per day. If you need a higher limit or want to lower your limit for safety, you can request a change through the mobile app or by calling customer care at 1800-123-4567.

title: How do I manage or cancel a UPI AutoPay mandate?
content: UPI AutoPay lets you set up recurring payments such as subscriptions, SIPs, EMIs, and bill payments that are debited automatically. To view, pause, or cancel a UPI AutoPay mandate, open your UPI app, go to the UPI AutoPay or Mandates section, and you will see a list of all active mandates with their amount and frequency. Select the mandate you want to manage and choose Pause or Cancel. Once cancelled, no further automatic debits will happen for that mandate. If you see an AutoPay mandate you did not authorise, cancel it immediately and report it to Pratham Bank customer care at 1800-123-4567, as it could be linked to a fraudulent subscription. You can also set spending limits on mandates for added safety.

title: How do I block my lost or stolen debit card?
content: If your debit card is lost, stolen, or you suspect misuse, block it immediately to prevent fraud. You can block your Pratham Bank debit card instantly in the mobile app under Cards, then Block or Freeze Card. You can also call the 24x7 customer care at 1800-123-4567 and select the card-blocking option, or send an SMS in the format BLOCK followed by the last four digits of your card to 567676 from your registered mobile number. Blocking is immediate and permanent for that card. After blocking, you can request a replacement card through the app or your branch, which is usually delivered within seven working days. If any unauthorised transaction has already happened, also raise a dispute and report it within three days to limit your liability.

title: Can I temporarily freeze my card instead of blocking it?
content: Yes. If you have only misplaced your card and may find it, you can temporarily freeze or lock it instead of permanently blocking it. In the Pratham Bank mobile app, go to Cards, select the card, and choose Freeze or Lock Card. While frozen, no transactions will go through, but you can unlock it instantly in the app once you find the card, without needing a replacement. You can also separately control where the card works by enabling or disabling online transactions, international usage, ATM withdrawals, and contactless tap payments, and by setting custom transaction limits. This gives you fine-grained control over your card's security.

title: How do I dispute an unknown charge on my debit or credit card?
content: If you see a transaction on your debit or credit card statement that you did not make, dispute it as soon as possible. First freeze or block the card to stop further misuse. Then raise a dispute through the Pratham Bank app under Cards, then Dispute a Transaction, or call customer care at 1800-123-4567. Provide the transaction date, amount, and merchant name. The bank will initiate a chargeback process with the card network and the merchant's bank, which can take a few weeks to resolve. For unauthorised transactions reported promptly, RBI's limited liability rules apply, and you may not be held liable if you reported quickly and were not negligent. Keep any evidence such as messages or emails related to the transaction.

title: How do I check my account balance?
content: You can check your Pratham Bank account balance in several ways. In the mobile app or internet banking, your balance is shown on the home screen after you log in. You can give a missed call to 1800-555-0000 from your registered mobile number to receive your balance by SMS. You can also send an SMS in the format BAL to 567676 from your registered mobile number. At any Pratham Bank ATM, insert your card, enter your PIN, and select Balance Enquiry. For a mini statement of your last few transactions, use the Mini Statement option in the app, give a missed call to 1800-555-0001, or use the mini statement option at an ATM. Balance enquiry through missed call and SMS is free and works even without internet.

title: How do I update my KYC details?
content: Know Your Customer, or KYC, updation is a periodic requirement set by RBI to keep your account details current. To update your KYC with Pratham Bank, you can use the mobile app or internet banking under Profile, then Update KYC, and upload your identity and address proof such as Aadhaar and PAN. You can also visit your branch with original and photocopies of your documents. Commonly accepted documents include Aadhaar card, PAN card, passport, voter ID, and driving licence. For minor changes, video KYC may be available within the app. Be careful: Pratham Bank will never send you a link by SMS or WhatsApp asking you to update KYC urgently or your account will be blocked. Such messages are a common fraud. Always update KYC only through official channels and never share OTPs while doing so.

title: How do I change the mobile number linked to my account?
content: To change or update the mobile number registered with your Pratham Bank account, the most secure way is to visit your nearest branch with your identity proof and submit a mobile number change request; the new number is usually updated within one to two working days. If your existing registered number is still active, you may also be able to update your number at a Pratham Bank ATM by selecting the Mobile Number Registration or Update option, or through internet banking after additional verification. Your registered mobile number is critical because all OTPs and transaction alerts are sent to it, so keep it up to date, especially if you have changed your SIM or number. Never let anyone else's number be linked to your account.

title: How do I open a new savings account?
content: You can open a new Pratham Bank savings account online or at a branch. To open online, download the Pratham Bank mobile app, choose Open Savings Account, and complete the digital process using your Aadhaar and PAN for instant e-KYC, then complete video KYC with an executive. To open at a branch, carry your Aadhaar, PAN, a passport-size photograph, and address proof. You will need to maintain the minimum balance applicable to the account type you choose; zero-balance and basic savings accounts are also available, including accounts under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana for financial inclusion. Once your account is opened, you will receive your account number, and you can request a debit card, cheque book, and set up internet and mobile banking.

title: How do I reset my internet banking or mobile banking password?
content: If you have forgotten your internet banking or mobile banking password, you can reset it yourself. On the Pratham Bank login page or app, tap Forgot Password, then verify your identity using your customer ID or account number, your registered mobile number for OTP, and your debit card details or date of birth. After verification, you can set a new password. For security, choose a strong password that you do not use anywhere else, and never share it. If you are repeatedly unable to reset, or you think someone else has accessed your account, call customer care at 1800-123-4567 to lock your online banking access and get help. Never enter your password on a website reached through a link in an SMS or email.

title: I am unable to log in to mobile banking. What should I do?
content: If you cannot log in to the Pratham Bank mobile app, first check that you have a working internet connection and that the app is updated to the latest version from the official app store. If you entered the wrong password or MPIN several times, your access may be temporarily locked for security; wait for the lockout period to end or use the Forgot Password or Forgot MPIN option to reset. Make sure your phone's date and time are set automatically, as incorrect time can cause OTP and login failures. If you changed your phone or SIM, you may need to re-register the app using OTP on your registered mobile number. If the problem continues, call customer care at 1800-123-4567 for assistance. Avoid logging in on public or shared devices.

title: How do I request a new cheque book?
content: You can request a new cheque book through the Pratham Bank mobile app or internet banking under Services, then Request Cheque Book, where you can choose the number of leaves and confirm your delivery address. You can also request a cheque book at an ATM under the Services menu, or by visiting your branch. The cheque book is usually delivered by post or courier to your registered address within seven to ten working days. You will receive an SMS update when it is dispatched. Always keep your cheque book in a safe place, never sign blank cheques, and report a lost cheque book to the bank immediately so the relevant cheque numbers can be stopped to prevent misuse.

title: How do I check my loan EMI, due date, or outstanding amount?
content: To check details of your Pratham Bank loan, including your EMI amount, next due date, outstanding principal, and amortisation schedule, log in to the mobile app or internet banking and go to the Loans section, where each active loan is listed. You can also download your loan statement and interest certificate from there. For any change such as updating the EMI debit account, requesting a payment holiday, or foreclosing the loan, you can raise a request in the app or call customer care at 1800-123-4567. Make sure the account from which the EMI is auto-debited has sufficient balance before the due date to avoid bounce charges and an impact on your credit score. If you face difficulty in repayment, contact the bank early to discuss options.

title: How do I apply for a personal loan?
content: You can apply for a Pratham Bank personal loan through the mobile app, internet banking, or by visiting a branch. Many existing customers have a pre-approved loan offer that can be availed instantly in the app under Loans, with the amount credited to the account within minutes after digital verification. For a fresh application, you will typically need identity proof, address proof, PAN, and income documents such as salary slips or bank statements. Eligibility and interest rate depend on your income, credit score, and relationship with the bank. Always review the interest rate, processing fee, tenure, and total repayment before accepting. Beware of fraudsters offering instant loans through unknown apps or links and asking for advance fees; always apply only through official Pratham Bank channels.

title: What are the official ways to contact Pratham Bank customer support?
content: You can reach Pratham Bank customer support through several official channels. The 24x7 toll-free customer care number is 1800-123-4567 for general banking, cards, and fraud reporting. For account balance, you can give a missed call to 1800-555-0000. You can chat with the assistant or raise a service request inside the mobile app and internet banking. You can email support at care@prathambank.example. You can also visit any Pratham Bank branch during working hours. For UPI or banking fraud, additionally call the national cyber crime helpline 1930 and report at cybercrime.gov.in. Always verify the customer care number from the bank's official website or the back of your debit card, and never trust customer care numbers found through random search results or social media, as these are often fake numbers run by fraudsters.

title: How do I escalate a complaint if it is not resolved?
content: If your complaint with Pratham Bank is not resolved within the promised time, you can escalate it. First, note your complaint reference number and follow up with customer care at 1800-123-4567. If still unresolved, write to the bank's Grievance Redressal Officer or Nodal Officer, whose details are published on the bank's website. If you do not receive a satisfactory response within thirty days, or your complaint is rejected, you can approach the RBI Ombudsman under the Reserve Bank Integrated Ombudsman Scheme by filing a complaint online at the RBI's complaint management system portal, cms.rbi.org.in, or by calling the RBI contact centre at 14448. The ombudsman service is free of cost. Keep all complaint references, dates, and communication records ready when you escalate.

title: What are the bank's working hours and how do I find a branch or ATM?
content: Pratham Bank branches are generally open from 10 in the morning to 4 in the evening on weekdays, and from 10 in the morning to 2 in the afternoon on the first, third, and fifth Saturdays of the month. Branches remain closed on the second and fourth Saturdays, on Sundays, and on public and bank holidays as notified. To find your nearest branch or ATM, use the Locate Us or Branch and ATM Locator feature in the mobile app or on the bank's official website by entering your city or PIN code. ATMs and digital banking through the app and internet banking are available 24x7, so most services such as balance enquiry, fund transfer, card blocking, and cheque book requests can be done any time without visiting a branch.

title: Money was debited but the transaction failed — when will I get it back?
content: If your account was debited but the payment failed or did not reach the beneficiary, do not worry; the amount is usually reversed automatically. As per the Reserve Bank of India's Turn Around Time (TAT) framework for failed transactions, the money for a failed UPI or card transaction is auto-reversed to your account, typically within one to five working days depending on the type of transaction. For example, a failed UPI payment where the account was debited but the beneficiary did not receive it should be auto-reversed within one working day. If the reversal does not happen within the prescribed time, the bank is liable to pay you compensation of one hundred rupees per day of delay, and you should raise a complaint with Pratham Bank customer care at 1800-123-4567 quoting the transaction reference number. Check your account statement after a day before raising a complaint, as the credit often appears automatically.

title: How long does a refund take to be credited to my account?
content: When a merchant such as an e-commerce site processes a refund, the money does not arrive instantly. A refund first has to be initiated by the merchant, after which it passes through the payment gateway and the banking system before it is credited to your account. For UPI and card refunds, this typically takes three to seven working days from the date the merchant initiates the refund. If your refund is showing as processing or pending in your statement, it means the bank has received the instruction and the credit is on its way. If a refund does not arrive within seven working days of the merchant confirming it, contact the merchant for the Acquirer Reference Number (ARN), then share it with Pratham Bank customer care at 1800-123-4567 so the credit can be traced.

title: When is my monthly pension credited and what if it is delayed?
content: Monthly pension is usually credited to your account at the beginning of each month, often on or around the first working day, once the disbursing authority such as the treasury or the pension office releases it. Occasionally the credit can be delayed by a few days due to processing at the government or treasury end, or if your annual life certificate (Jeevan Pramaan) is pending. If your pension is showing as processing or pending in your account, it has been initiated and should be credited shortly. If it is significantly delayed, first ensure your life certificate and KYC are up to date, then contact Pratham Bank customer care at 1800-123-4567 or your pension disbursing authority. Pratham Bank does not control the date on which the pension is released by the government.

title: What is the current home loan interest rate?
content: Pratham Bank home loan interest rates currently start from around 8.40 percent per annum (indicative) for eligible salaried borrowers, and are linked to an external benchmark, the repo rate, so they move when the RBI revises the repo rate. The exact rate offered depends on your credit score, loan amount, tenure, and whether you are a salaried or self-employed applicant. Home loans are available for tenures up to thirty years with a processing fee that is a small percentage of the loan amount. You can use the EMI calculator in the mobile app to estimate your monthly instalment. These figures are indicative; please confirm the latest applicable rate and offer through the app, a branch, or customer care at 1800-123-4567 before applying.

title: What is the personal loan interest rate?
content: Pratham Bank personal loan interest rates are indicative and typically range from about 10.50 percent to 18 percent per annum, depending on your credit score, income, and relationship with the bank. Personal loans are unsecured, so the rate is higher than a secured loan such as a home loan. Pre-approved customers often get the best rates and instant disbursal through the mobile app. Tenures usually range from one to five years. There is a one-time processing fee. Always check the rate, processing fee, and total repayment before accepting, and avoid unofficial loan apps that promise instant loans and ask for advance fees, as these are often fraudulent.

title: What are the fixed deposit (FD) interest rates?
content: Pratham Bank fixed deposit interest rates are indicative and currently range from about 3.50 percent per annum for short tenures to around 7.25 percent per annum for the most rewarding tenure buckets, typically between one and three years. Senior citizens usually earn an additional 0.50 percent over the standard rate. Interest can be paid out periodically or compounded and paid at maturity. Tax-saving fixed deposits with a five-year lock-in are also available. You can open an FD instantly in the mobile app and choose your tenure and payout option. These rates are indicative and subject to change; confirm the latest rates in the app or at a branch before investing.

title: What is the savings account interest rate and minimum balance requirement?
content: Pratham Bank savings accounts earn interest at an indicative rate of around 3.00 to 3.50 percent per annum, calculated on the daily closing balance and credited quarterly. Regular savings accounts require an average monthly balance, commonly around five thousand rupees in metro and urban branches and lower in semi-urban and rural branches, and a charge applies if the balance falls below this. Basic savings accounts and accounts under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana have no minimum balance requirement. You can check the specific requirement for your account type in the mobile app. These figures are indicative and may change.

title: What are the charges and limits for NEFT, IMPS, RTGS and UPI?
content: For Pratham Bank customers, fund transfers done through the mobile app or internet banking are largely free. NEFT and RTGS transactions initiated online have no charge as per RBI guidelines. IMPS may carry a small charge for higher amounts at some banks, though it is often free online. UPI payments are free for customers. RTGS is meant for high-value transfers of two lakh rupees and above, while NEFT and IMPS can be used for smaller amounts, with IMPS available instantly 24x7. UPI has a standard per-transaction limit of up to one lakh rupees, with higher limits for certain verified categories. Branch-initiated transfers may attract a service charge. Confirm the latest charges in the app or at a branch.
